rulings.Case Title: CEDRIC BERMOND VS. JOSE TORRES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 24, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT JOSE TORRES MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is CONTINUED to May 19, 2023, to be heard with the Demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CEDRIC BERMOND VS. JOSE TORRES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT JOSE TORRES DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 24, 2023 line 1. is CONTINUED to May 19, 2023, for supplemental briefing. Defendant to provide a supplemental brief to provide authority supporting the proposition that abatement, pursuant to CCP 430.10(c), of a cause of action in an earlier-filed complaint (Case No. 603950) is proper, and that abatement is not limited to subsequently-filed Complaints (Case No. 671276). Brief not to exceed three pages, due no later than May 2, 2023. Plaintiff's response, not to exceed three pages, due no later than May 9, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
